grammar.issues: Overall grammar is strong, but some sentences are very long and could benefit from better punctuation or splitting for readability.

tone.description: Professional and appropriate for a technical developer resume. Some sections feel overly sparse due to presentation style.

suggestions: Remove excessive spacing between letters and words; this style negatively impacts readability and professional appearance.
Reformat the resume to standard structure: include clear headers, bullet points for skills and experiences, and proper paragraph/line breaks.
In the contact section, present phone and email in a standard format without spaces.
Expand the 'Profile' section into concise sentences with clearer punctuation to improve readability.
Add more details on experience and projects such as dates, roles, achievements to enhance clarity and relevance.
Consider including a dedicated section for education and certifications with dates.
Replace generic terms like 'Various animated websites' with specific project descriptions or links if possible.
Avoid full capitalization of section headers and name to improve visual flow.
Consider using bullet points for skills and technologies rather than listing them in spaced-out text.
Correct minor grammar style issues by breaking up very long sentences for better reader comprehension.
